docs.title: RPC Operator User Documentation
docs.text: Welcome to the RPC Operator documentation. This guide covers installation, configuration, pipeline authoring, and operations.

docs.title: Quick Links
docs.text:
• Install the Operator
  • Deploy Your First Pipeline
  • Pipeline CRD Reference
  • PipelineCluster CRD Reference

docs.title: What is the RPC Operator?
docs.text:
The RPC Operator is a Kubernetes operator for managing Redpanda Connect pipelines. It provides:

  • Simple deployment of data pipelines as Kubernetes resources
  • Native K8s integration via Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
  • Multi-cluster support via PipelineCluster for distributed stream processing
  • Built-in monitoring with Prometheus integration

docs.title: For Platform Administrators
docs.text: See the Operating section for deployment, authentication, monitoring, and cluster configuration.

docs.title: For Pipeline Authors
docs.text: See the Authoring section for pipeline configuration, processors, input/output connectors, and error handling.

docs.title: Pipeline Resources
docs.text: A Pipeline CRD defines a Redpanda Connect configuration.

docs.title: Configuration Structure
docs.text: Raw YAML pipelines consist of input, processors, and output.

docs.title: Processors
docs.text: Transform and route data through processing steps.

docs.title: Stream Pipelines
docs.text: Deploy pipelines as streams on the cluster.

docs.title: What is a PipelineCluster?
docs.text: A PipelineCluster is a group of pods running interconnected Redpanda Connect instances for distributed stream processing.

docs.title: Stream Pipelines
docs.text: Pipelines deployed on a cluster run in stream mode.

docs.title: Scaling
docs.text: Scale clusters and pipelines for throughput and resilience.

docs.title: Configure a Cluster
docs.text: Configure the RPC Operator for your Kubernetes environment.

docs.title: Deploy Your First Pipeline
docs.text: Create and deploy a simple pipeline in minutes.

docs.title: A Basic Pipeline
docs.text: A pipeline configuration consists of an input, optional processors, and an output.

docs.title: Install the Operator
docs.text: This page covers installing the RPC Operator on your Kubernetes cluster.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Kubernetes 1.11+
  • Helm 3.0+ (for Helm-based installation)
  • kubectl configured for your cluster

docs.title: Using Helm
docs.text:
bash helm install rpc-operator ./charts/rpc-operator \ -n rpc-operator-system --create-namespace

Refer to the Helm chart values.yaml in charts/rpc-operator/ for configuration options and examples.

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text: Secure access to pipelines via Bearer token or OIDC.

docs.title: Token Authentication
docs.text: Use Kubernetes ServiceAccount tokens for pipeline access.

docs.text: Deploy the RPC Operator to your cluster.

docs.text: Use Helm to deploy the operator to your cluster.

docs.text: Configure operator behavior via Helm values or environment variables.

docs.title: Pipeline CRD Reference
docs.text: Complete reference for the Pipeline Custom Resource Definition.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The Pipeline CRD defines a Redpanda Connect pipeline and its lifecycle on Kubernetes.

docs.title: Metadata
docs.text:
• name (string, required): Unique name for the pipeline
  • namespace (string): Kubernetes namespace (defaults to default)

docs.title: Status
docs.text:
• phase (string): Current lifecycle phase (Pending, Running, Failed, Succeeded)
  • conditions: Array of pipeline conditions
  • observedGeneration: Generation of the most recently observed spec

docs.title: spec.rawYAML
docs.text:
Raw Redpanda Connect pipeline configuration as YAML string.

Type: string Required: yes

The rawYAML field contains the complete pipeline definition.

docs.title: PipelineCluster CRD Reference
docs.text: Complete reference for the PipelineCluster Custom Resource Definition.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The PipelineCluster CRD defines a cluster of Redpanda Connect instances for distributed stream processing.

docs.title: Metadata
docs.text:
• name (string, required): Unique name for the cluster
  • namespace (string): Kubernetes namespace (defaults to default)

docs.title: Status
docs.text:
• phase (string): Current lifecycle phase
  • instances: Number of running cluster instances
  • conditions: Array of cluster conditions
  • observedGeneration: Generation of the most recently observed spec

docs.title: spec.replicas
docs.text:
Number of cluster pod instances.

Type: integer Default: 1

docs.title: spec.image
docs.text:
Container image for cluster instances.

Type: string Default: Latest operator-managed image
